overall_verdict.verdict_reason: The association has outstanding delinquent assessment receivables from homeowners and has experienced historical issues with mold and deteriorating asphalt, though it maintains a substantial reserve fund balance.

findings.label: Delinquent Assessment Receivables
findings.finding: The association reports an aggregate total of $215.12 in common expense assessments that are past due over thirty days by homeowners [source: ResaleCert.txt, Section 6].
findings.buyer_note: You may face higher dues if other owners continue to default on their assessments.

findings.label: Active Unit Mold Issues
findings.finding: Board meeting minutes indicate that Unit 21 and potentially other units are experiencing active mold issues that require management intervention [source: RegularMeetingMinutes.txt, Page 1].
findings.buyer_note: Unresolved mold issues in the building could lead to costly remediation assessments.

findings.label: Deteriorating Asphalt Infrastructure
findings.finding: The community asphalt has deteriorated, causing cracks in various places, requiring a contractor assessment and warranty review [source: RegularMeetingMinutes.txt, Page 2].
findings.buyer_note: Asphalt repairs may require funding from reserves or a future special assessment.

findings.label: Mandatory Carbon Monoxide Detector Installation
findings.finding: State law requires sellers to install operational carbon monoxide detectors prior to selling, which the association does not inspect or verify [source: OtherDocument.txt, Page 1].
findings.buyer_note: You must verify that the seller has installed a carbon monoxide detector before closing.

notable_absences: The resale certificate does not explicitly contain the conspicuous statutory warning notice regarding the absence of a reserve study required by RCW 64.90.640(1)(y), although a reserve fund balance is disclosed.

action_items.action: Request verification from the seller that an operational carbon monoxide detector has been installed in the unit.
Inquire with the board regarding the status of mold remediation for Unit 21 and whether common elements are affected.
Request the latest reserve study to evaluate if the $617,808.70 reserve balance is sufficient to cover upcoming asphalt and painting projects.
